workflow.name: Get enriched location, weather, and timezone data using free APIs
workflow.description:
# Get location insights using free APIs

Transform GPS coordinates into rich location data using 100% free APIs. This webhook aggregates address, timezone, weather, and sun data into a single response. Perfect for location tracking apps, travel platforms, and IoT projects.

## What You'll Get

A single API endpoint returning 28 enriched data fields:
- Detailed address with components (suburb, city, state, country, postcode)
- Timezone data (name, abbreviation, current local time and date)
- Live weather (temperature, humidity, pressure, conditions with icon)
- Sun times (sunrise, sunset, day length)
- Visual assets (weather icons and country flag URLs)

## How It Works

1. Webhook receives coordinates via GET request (lat and lon parameters)
2. Parallel API calls to 4 free services (OpenStreetMap, TimezoneDB, Sunrise-Sunset, OpenWeatherMap)
3. Data merging combines all responses
4. Format and structure transforms data into 28 clean fields
5. JSON response returns enriched location data

Response time: 1 to 3 seconds

## Set Up Steps

### 1. Get Free API Keys
- OpenWeatherMap: Sign up at openweathermap.org (free tier: 60 calls/min)
- TimezoneDB: Register at timezonedb.com (free tier available)
- Note: OpenStreetMap and Sunrise-Sunset require no keys

### 2. Configure Credentials
- Add OpenWeatherMap credentials to the OpenWeatherMap node
- Replace TimezoneDB API key in HTTP Request node with your key

### 3. Activate and Test
- Activate workflow to generate webhook URL
- Test with sample: ?lat=27.1751495&lon=78.0395673

## Example Usage

**Request:**
```bash
curl "https://your-n8n.com/webhook/geo-details?lat=27.1751495&lon=78.0395673"
```

**Response:** JSON array with 28 fields including address, timezone, weather, and sun data.

## Use Cases

- Location tracking applications
- Travel and tourism platforms
- Weather dashboards
- Fleet management systems
- Geographic analytics tools
- Smart home automation
- Educational projects

## Why This Template?

- Zero cost (all APIs are free)
- Production ready with error handling
- Fast response via parallel processing
- Well documented with sticky notes
- Easy to customize and extend
- No vendor lock-in

Perfect for startups, indie developers, students, and budget-conscious projects.

## Rate Limits (Free Tiers)

- OpenStreetMap: 1 req/sec
- TimezoneDB: 1 req/sec
- OpenWeatherMap: 60 calls/min, 1M calls/month
- Sunrise-Sunset: No documented limits

Tip: Implement caching to stay within limits.

**Tags:** location, geocoding, weather, timezone, free-api, webhook, rest-api, gps

workflow.name: Get location insights using free APIs

nodes.name: Merge
nodes.notes:
Merges 4 parallel API responses into single object.
Input Sources:
1. HTTP Request (Nominatim) - Address data
2. HTTP Request1 (TimezoneDB) - Timezone data
3. HTTP Request2 (Sunrise-Sunset) - Sun times
4. OpenWeatherMap - Weather data
Merge Mode: Combine by position
Clash Handling: Add suffix to duplicate fields
Output: Single object with all data combined.

nodes.name: OpenWeatherMap
nodes.notes:
OpenWeatherMap API - Current weather conditions.
Cost: FREE tier (requires free API key)
Rate Limit: 60 calls/min, 1M calls/month
Returns:
- Temperature (current, min, max, feels like)
- Atmospheric pressure & humidity
- Weather condition & description
- Weather icon URL
Free tier is more than sufficient for most use cases.
Credentials stored in n8n: "OpenWeatherMap account"

nodes.name: HTTP Request (Nominatim)
nodes.notes:
OpenStreetMap Nominatim API - Converts coordinates to address.
API: https://nominatim.openstreetmap.org/reverse
Cost: 100% FREE (no API key required)
Rate Limit: 1 request/second
Returns:
- Full address (display_name)
- Address components (suburb, state, country, etc.)
- Postcode
Usage Policy: Fair use, max 1 req/sec

nodes.name: Webhook - Accepts Coordinates
nodes.notes:
Receives GPS coordinates via query parameters.
Required Parameters:
- lat: Latitude (-90 to 90)
- lon: Longitude (-180 to 180)
Example:
https://n8n.srinikb.in/webhook/geo-details?lat=27.1751495&lon=78.0395673
This triggers 3 parallel API calls for maximum efficiency.

nodes.name: HTTP Request (TimezoneDB)
nodes.notes:
TimezoneDB API - Gets timezone information.
API: http://api.timezonedb.com/v2.1/get-time-zone
Cost: FREE tier (requires free API key)
Rate Limit: 1 request/second
Returns:
- Timezone name (e.g., "Asia/Kolkata")
- Abbreviation (e.g., "IST")
- Current local time & date
- Unix timestamp
- Country code

API key required (Free tier)

nodes.name: HTTP Request (Sunrise-Sunset)
nodes.notes:
Sunrise-Sunset.org API - Calculates astronomical times.
API: https://api.sunrise-sunset.org/json
Cost: 100% FREE (no API key required)
Rate Limit: No documented limit (fair use)
Returns:
- Sunrise time (local timezone)
- Sunset time (local timezone)
- Day length
Depends on: Timezone data from HTTP Request1
Uses timezone ID for accurate local time conversion.

nodes.name: Edit Fields - Format & Structure Output
nodes.notes:
Transforms merged data into clean, user-friendly format.
Creates 28 structured fields:
- Location: lat, lon, address components
- Time: timezone, current time/date, timestamp
- Sun: sunrise, sunset, day length
- Weather: temperature, conditions, icons
- Visual: country flag URL, weather icon URL
Formatting:
- Time: 12-hour format (hh:mm a)
- Date: dd-MMM-yyyy format
- Dot notation enabled for nested fields
This is the final data structure sent to client.

nodes.name: Respond to Webhook - Return JSON Response
nodes.notes:
Sends formatted data back to client.
Response:
- Status: 200 OK
- Format: JSON array
- Content: All enriched location data
The response contains 28 fields with complete location intelligence including address, timezone, weather, and astronomical data.
Total processing time: ~1-3 seconds
All data from 100% FREE APIs! 🎉

parameters.content:
## Get location insights using free APIs
This workflow transforms GPS coordinates into rich location intelligence using free public APIs.
It aggregates data from OpenStreetMap, TimezoneDB, OpenWeatherMap, and Sunrise-Sunset.org to provide address details, timezone, weather, and astronomical times in a single call.

## How it works
1. **Input**: Accepts `lat` and `lon` via webhook.
2. **Process**: Fetches data from 4 parallel sources.
3. **Output**: Returns a unified JSON object with 28 enriched fields.

## Setup steps
1. **Configure Webhook**: Set the path to `geo-details`.
2. **Add Credentials**: Add your OpenWeatherMap and TimezoneDB free API keys.
3. **Deploy**: Activate the workflow and use the production URL.

## Sample Request & Response
Location: Taj Mahal, Agra, India

REQUEST:
```
curl "https://n8n.example.com/webhook/geo-details?lat=27.1751495&lon=78.0395673"
```


RESPONSE: JSON
```
[
  {
    "lat": 27.1751495,
    "lon": 78.0395673,
    "timezone": "Asia/Kolkata",
    "timezone_short": "IST",
    "current_time": "10:25 PM",
    "current_date": "15-Feb-2026",
    "timestamp": "1771194349",
    "address_full": "Cicuit House Road, Taj Ganj, Agra, Uttar Pradesh, 282004, India",
    "neighbourhood": null,
    "suburb": "Taj Ganj",
    "town": null,
    "county": null,
    "state_district": "Agra",
    "state": "Uttar Pradesh",
    "country": "India",
    "country_code": "in",
    "postcode": "282004",
    "sunrise": "6:53:17 AM",
    "sunset": "6:10:35 PM",
    "day_length": "11:17:18",
    "temperature": "18.96",
    "feels_like": "17.85",
    "temp_min": "18.96",
    "temp_max": "18.96",
    "pressure": "1014",
    "humidity": "36",
    "weather_main": "Clear",
    "weather_description": "clear sky",
    "weather_icon": "https://openweathermap.org/img/wn/01n@2x.png",
    "country_flag": "https://flagsapi.com/IN/flat/64.png"
  }
]
```
Note: Returns 28 fields with complete location data from 100% FREE APIs

main.node: Respond to Webhook - Return JSON Response
